data.title: EURO STOXX 50 Index Ends the Week 3.15% Higher at 5493.40 — Data Talk
data.description:
The EURO STOXX 50 Index is up 168.00 points or 3.15% this week to 5493.40

  • Largest one week point and percentage gain since the week ending Jan 17, 2025
  • Up for six consecutive weeks
  • Up 621.95 points or 12.77% over the last six weeks
  • Largest six week point gain since the week ending Nov. 2, 2001
  • Largest six week percentage gain since the week ending Dec. 2, 2022
  • Longest winning streak since the week ending March 28, 2024 when the market rose for 10 straight weeks
  • Up seven of the past eight weeks
  • Today it is down 7.10 points or 0.13%
  • Second highest close in history
  • Largest one day point and percentage decline since Friday, Feb. 7, 2025
  • Snaps a four trading day winning streak
  • Off 0.13% from its record close of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Today's closing value is the second highest this year
  • Off 0.13% from its 52-week high of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Up 20.16% from its 52-week low of 4571.60 hit Monday, Aug. 5, 2024
  • Rose 15.27% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 0.13% from its 2025 closing high of 5500.50 hit Thursday, Feb. 13, 2025
  • Up 12.77% from its 2025 closing low of 4871.45 hit Friday, Jan. 3, 2025
  • Month-to-date it is up 3.91%
  • Year-to-date it is up 597.42 points or 12.20%

Source: Dow Jones Market Data, FactSet

data.title: GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO /OH/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company, a global leader in tire manufacturing and distribution, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. This summary highlights the key aspects of Goodyear's performance and strategic direction as outlined in the report.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $18,878 million, a decrease of 5.9% from $20,066 million in 2023, primarily due to lower tire volume and global declines in price and product mix.
  • Operating Income: $1,318 million, an increase of $350 million from $968 million in 2023, driven by benefits from the Goodyear Forward plan and lower raw material costs.
  • Net Income: $70 million, compared to a net loss of $689 million in 2023, mainly due to lower rationalization charges, higher segment operating income, and lower impairment charges.
  • Net Income Per Share: $0.24, compared to a net loss per share of $2.42 in 2023, reflecting improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through three regional segments: Americas, Europe, Middle East and Africa (EMEA), and Asia Pacific. Each segment focuses on the development, manufacture, distribution, and sale of tires and related products.
  • Geographical Performance: In 2024, the Americas was the largest segment in terms of revenue, with significant operations in the United States, Canada, Mexico, and South America. EMEA and Asia Pacific also contributed to the company's global footprint, with manufacturing facilities and sales operations across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia.
  • Sales Units: The company sold 166.6 million tire units worldwide in 2024, a decrease from 173.3 million units in 2023. The Americas segment sold 81.6 million units, EMEA sold 48.9 million units, and Asia Pacific sold 36.1 million units.
  • New Product Launches: In 2024, the company launched several new consumer and commercial tires across its brands, including the Goodyear Assurance WeatherReady 2, Goodyear ElectricDrive 2, and Cooper Discoverer Stronghold AT in the Americas. EMEA introduced the Goodyear Eagle F1 Asymmetric 6 and Dunlop All Season 2, while Asia Pacific released the Goodyear Wrangler Boulder MT and Mickey Thompson Baja Boss A/T.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects to continue its Goodyear Forward transformation plan, aiming for significant margin expansion and cost reductions by the end of 2025. It anticipates benefits from this plan to contribute approximately $750 million to segment operating income in 2025.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Goodyear Forward Transformation Plan: Goodyear announced the Goodyear Forward transformation plan aimed at optimizing its product portfolio, expanding segment operating margins, and reducing leverage to drive long-term shareholder value. This includes a strategic review of chemical operations, the Dunlop brand, and the global OTR tire business.
  • Divestitures and Agreements: The company completed the sale of its OTR tire business to Yokohama for $905 million and entered into a Dunlop Purchase Agreement with SRI for $526 million.
  • Capital Management: Goodyear had $810 million in cash and cash equivalents and $3,555 million of unused credit availability as of December 31, 2024. The company plans capital expenditures of approximately $950 million in 2025 and expects rationalization payments of about $400 million. Goodyear also called for the redemption of its 9.5% Senior Notes due 2025, following the sale of its OTR tire business.
  • Future Outlook: In 2025, Goodyear expects to achieve $750 million in benefits from the Goodyear Forward plan, with a focus on improving operating margins and reducing costs. The company anticipates raw material headwinds but plans to offset these through pricing actions and cost-saving initiatives. Goodyear also expects working capital to be a source of cash flow, contributing $100 million to $150 million in operating cash flows for the year.

Challenges and Risks

  • Implementation Risks: The success of the Goodyear Forward plan is contingent on overcoming challenges such as management focus, retention of key personnel, and unforeseen implementation costs. Additionally, the sale of the Dunlop brand and the OTR tire business introduces risks related to the separation of these businesses and the realization of expected benefits.
  • Market and Competitive Risks: Goodyear faces significant global competition, particularly from Bridgestone and Michelin, and must innovate to maintain market share. Economic downturns, inflation, and supply chain disruptions pose additional risks, potentially impacting raw material costs and operational efficiency.
  • Regulatory Risks: Compliance with environmental laws and potential changes in tariffs and trade agreements could affect the company's global operations.
  • Operational Risks: Management is focused on executing the Goodyear Forward plan to drive shareholder value, but acknowledges the risks associated with strategic initiatives and market conditions. The company is investing in capital improvements to modernize facilities and increase production of higher-margin tires, but these efforts may be disrupted by economic uncertainties and competitive pressures. Goodyear is also addressing labor relations challenges, particularly with unionized employees, which could impact operations if not managed effectively.
  • Market Risks: Goodyear is exposed to market risks such as foreign currency fluctuations, which have negatively impacted sales and operating income. The company also faces interest rate risks due to its variable rate debt, which could increase debt service obligations if rates rise. Additionally, geopolitical events, such as the conflict in Ukraine, have exacerbated supply chain disruptions and increased costs, posing further risks to the company's financial performance.

SEC Filing: GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER CO /OH/ [ GT ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: EASTMAN CHEMICAL CO SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Eastman Chemical Co, a global specialty materials company, has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing its financial and operational performance for the fiscal year. The report highlights the company's strategic initiatives, business achievements, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Eastman Chemical Co reported a solid financial performance for the fiscal year, with key metrics showing growth and stability:

  • Sales: $9,382 million, a 2% increase compared to 2023, driven by higher sales volume despite lower selling prices.
  • Gross Profit: $2,290 million, an 11% increase from 2023, attributed to higher sales volume and lower raw material and energy costs.
  • Earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT): $1,278 million, a slight decrease of 2% from 2023, primarily due to restructuring activities and other charges.
  • Net earnings attributable to Eastman: $905 million, reflecting a slight increase from $894 million in 2023.
  • Diluted EPS: $7.67, up from $7.49 in 2023, indicating improved earnings per share performance.

Business Highlights

Eastman Chemical Co's business performance was marked by growth in several key segments and regions:

  • Revenue Segments:
    • The Advanced Materials segment saw a 4% increase in sales revenue, driven by higher sales volume, particularly in premium interlayers products.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment experienced a 1% increase in sales revenue, attributed to higher sales volume in agriculture, personal care, aviation, and water treatment markets.
    • The Chemical Intermediates segment's sales revenue remained relatively unchanged, with lower selling prices offset by higher sales volume.
    • The Fibers segment reported a 2% increase in sales revenue, primarily due to higher selling prices in the acetate tow product line.
  • Geographical Performance: Sales revenue in the Asia Pacific region increased by 6%, driven by higher sales volume, particularly within the Advanced Materials segment.
  • New Product Launches:
    • The Advanced Materials segment launched Saflex™ LiteCarbon Clear and Saflex Evoca™, enhancing the sustainability and design potential of laminated glass and electric vehicle glazing.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment introduced Eastman Esmeri™, a biodegradable cellulosic biopolymer for personal care, and Solus™, a biodegradable paper coating additive for food-safe packaging.
    • The Additives & Functional Products segment launched electronic grade isopropyl alcohol (IPA) for U.S. semiconductor manufacturers, expanding the EastaPure™ electronic chemicals line.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue leveraging its innovation-driven growth model, focusing on sustainable growth through differentiated products and significant scale advantages in R&D and global market access.

Strategic Initiatives

Eastman Chemical Co is actively pursuing strategic initiatives to drive long-term growth and financial stability:

  • Innovation-Driven Growth: The company is focusing on molecular recycling technologies and differentiated application development, with significant investments in manufacturing capabilities in Europe, Middle East, Africa, and Asia Pacific.
  • Capital Management: Eastman has a $1.50 billion revolving credit facility, extended to February 2029. The company repaid $550 million in term loans in 2024 and repurchased 3,001,409 shares of common stock for $300 million under a $2.5 billion authorization. A cash dividend of $0.83 per share was declared for the first quarter of 2025.
  • Future Outlook: Expected capital spending between $700 million and $800 million in 2025, with a focus on maintaining a solid investment-grade credit rating to support long-term strategy and financial flexibility.

Challenges and Risks

Eastman Chemical Co faces several challenges and risks that could impact its business operations and financial performance:

  • Volatility in Raw Material and Energy Prices: Significant fluctuations in these costs pose a risk to the company's profitability. Eastman employs pricing and hedging strategies to mitigate these risks.
  • Environmental Costs and Liabilities: Changes in regulations or unanticipated government enforcement actions could result in higher costs, impacting the company's financial condition.
  • Pension and Postretirement Benefit Obligations: These obligations are influenced by discount rates, expected returns on plan assets, and demographic assumptions, which could lead to significant changes in expenses.
  • Market Risks: Eastman is exposed to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations, commodity price changes, and interest rate volatility. The company manages these risks through hedging strategies and derivative contracts.

SEC Filing: EASTMAN CHEMICAL CO [ EMN ] - 10-K - Feb. 14, 2025

data.title: Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Earnings
data.description:
COLDWATER, Mich., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. (OTC Pink: SOMC) announced fourth quarter net income of $2,650,000, or $0.57 per share, compared to net income of $2,437,000, or $0.54 per share, for the fourth quarter of 2023. Southern earned $10,402,000 or $2.28 per share, for the year ended December 31, 2024, compared with $10,905,000 or $2.40 per share, for the same period one year ago.

John R. Waldron, President and Chief Executive Officer of Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc., stated, “2024 was another solid year with total assets reaching approximately $1.5 billion. During the year ended December 31, 2024, total loans and deposits grew to $1.116 billion and $1.252 billion, respectively. While our earnings continue to be impacted by the current interest rate environment, we remain encouraged by the strength of our core deposits and our ability to maintain asset quality.”

The allowance for credit losses totaled $12,782,000, or 1.14% of loans on December 31, 2024, compared to $11,697,000, or 1.13% on December 31, 2023. Net loan charge-offs totaled $27,000 for 2024, compared to $15,000 for 2023. Non-performing loans as a percentage of total loans were 0.08% on December 31, 2024 and December 31, 2023.

The annualized return on average assets for the years ended December 31, 2024, and December 31, 2023, was 0.71% and 0.80%, respectively. The annualized return on average equity was 10.07% for 2024 compared to 11.94% for 2023. The tax equivalent net interest margin for the years ending December 31, 2024, and 2023 was 2.98% and 3.16%, respectively.

Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. is a bank holding company and the parent company of Southern Michigan Bank & Trust. It operates 18 offices within Branch, Calhoun, Hillsdale, Jackson, Kalamazoo and St. Joseph Counties providing a broad range of consumer, business and wealth management services throughout the region.

This press release contains forward-looking statements that are based on management’s beliefs, assumptions, current expectations, estimates and projections about the financial services industry, the economy, and Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. Forward-looking statements are identifiable by words or phrases such as “expected,” “begin,” and other similar words or expressions. All statements with reference to a future time period are forward-looking. Management’s determination of the provision and allowance for credit losses and other accounting estimates, such as the carrying value of goodwill, other real estate owned, mortgage servicing rights and the fair value of investment securities, involves judgments that are inherently forward-looking. The future effect of changes in the financial and credit markets and the national and regional economy on the banking industry, generally, and Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc., specifically, are also inherently uncertain. These statements are not guarantees of future performance and involve certain risks, uncertainties and assumptions ("risk factors") that are difficult to predict with regard to timing, extend, likelihood and degree of occurrence. Therefore, actual results and outcomes may materially differ from what may be expressed in or implied by such forward-looking statements. Southern Michigan Bancorp, Inc. does not undertake to update forward-looking statements to reflect the impact of circumstances or events that may arise after the date of the forward-looking statements.

data.title: Trump Administration Reviews CHIPS Act Awards, Potential Delays Expected
data.description:
The Trump administration is reviewing funding agreements under the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act, with potential revisions to award terms and possible delays in disbursements to semiconductor companies, Reuters said, citing industry sources.

The government is reviewing more criteria related to CHIPS Act financing, including clauses requiring the participation of unionized personnel in manufacturing projects and the availability of reasonably priced daycare for staff members. Still, another important issue is the degree of Chinese investment made by government subsidy beneficiaries.

The Chief Executive Officer of GlobalWafers, Doris Hsu, acknowledged the business has obtained $406 million in CHIPS Act money but has not yet received payments as it needs to reach certain benchmarks. Construction at the three sites continues as the company's U.S. growth plans remain unaltered despite continuous scrutiny.

Intel Corp. (INTC, Financials), which received $7.86 billion in direct support under the CHIPS Act, revealed receiving $2.2 billion but did not comment on the administration's evaluation. The business announced in October 2024 a $300 million investment in a Chinese plant. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing claimed to have received $1.5 billion prior to the new government's inauguration but did not specify more on possible financing changes.

data.title: European Equities Close Mostly Lower Friday; Eurozone Q4 Employment Growth Slows
data.description:
European stock markets closed mostly lower Friday as the FTSE 100 in London declined 0.37%, Germany's DAX 40 fell 0.82%, the Stoxx Europe 600 dropped 0.24%, the Swiss Market Index retreated 0.84%, while France's CAC 40 rose 0.18%.

The euro area's seasonally adjusted gross domestic product rose 0.1% in Q4, following a 0.4% increase in Q3, according to Eurostat. On a yearly basis, the region's economy grew 0.7%.

The number of employed individuals in the euro area rose 0.1% in Q4, compared with a 0.2% increase in Q3, Eurostat data showed. On a yearly basis, employment in the region was up 0.6% in the quarter.

In corporate news, HSBC Holdings (HSBC) is set to unveil $1.5 billion in annual cost savings as part of Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery's restructuring efforts, the Financial Times reported, citing two people familiar with the matter.

HSBC is preparing for a new round of layoffs within its investment banking division as part of Elhedery's restructuring plans, Bloomberg News reported, citing sources familiar with the matter.

Sky News reported Thursday that NatWest Group (NWG) is facing a potential criminal case over the debanking scandal as Reform UK leader Nigel Farage is considering filing criminal proceedings.

Nvidia (NVDA) said in a regulatory filing it has reduced its holdings in British semiconductor firm Arm Holdings (ARM) by 44%.

Amazon (AMZN) and three of its executives are under investigation by Italian prosecutors over alleged tax evasion worth 1.2 billion euros ($1.26 billion), according to Italian media reports.

data.title: Correction: Mobimo Posts Higher FY24 Attributable Profit, Revenue
data.description:
(Corrects revenue direction in the first paragraph)

Mobimo said Friday its attributable profit for full-year 2024 increased as revenue jumped year over year.

Profit attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 125.2 million francs, compared with 46.6 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 17.26 francs from 6.43 francs.

The Swiss residential property manager's revenue was 255.4 million francs, compared with 234 million francs a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board will propose a dividend of 10.25 francs per share at its annual general meeting on March 31. It paid 10.00 francs per share a year ago.

The company anticipates slightly lower rental income in 2025 compared with 2024, with growth projected to resume in 2026. Meanwhile, the 2025 net income from development projects and the sale of trading properties is expected to exceed 2024.

data.title: Republic Services Reconsiders Pricing Strategy For Municipal Contracts — Market Talk
data.description: Republic Services' pricing is coming down as its cost inflation drops, CEO Jon Vander Ark says on a call with analysts. The waste management company's retention rate remains very healthy as it gives customers a price point that fosters loyalty, Vander Ark says. But one area the company isn't pleased with is the municipal space. "That's one on the restricted side, and that's just an area of the business in general where customers on balance are not paying their fair share," he says. "We put an enormous amount of innovation, capital and labor into that equation, and we have many attractive customers and contracts, but not all of them meet our standard," the executive says. "You'll continue to see us optimize that portfolio over the next few years." Shares rise 2.7% to $230.61. (sabela.ojea@wsj.com; @sabelaojeaguix)

data.title: Diamondback Energy in Talks to Acquire Double Eagle, WSJ Says
IFC chief says it is doubling down on Pakistan, eyeing large infrastructure financing
data.description:
Key points:
  • IFC chief says $2 billion annual investment in Pakistan not hard target
  • Eyeing investment in infrastructure, agriculture, digital and financial sectors
  • Country needs investment in big projects such as airports, energy and ports

The World Bank's private investment arm is increasing equity investments and eyeing large-scale infrastructure financing in Pakistan, in an investment plan that could unlock $2 billion annually over a decade, the institution's chief told Reuters on Friday.

International Finance Corporation chief Makhtar Diop's maiden visit to Pakistan follows the World Bank's plans to allocate up to $20 billion for Pakistan under a Country Partnership Framework announced in January, with the IFC also slotted to invest the same amount.

"Between now and maybe October we will be able to progress enough on a couple of transactions that will signal that this is a country ready to receive large-scale financing for critical and important infrastructure," said Makhtar Diop, the corporation's managing director.

Diop said a $2 billion annual investment "is not a large number" for Pakistan, which needs infrastructure development in international airports, energy, water and ports.

Cash-strapped Pakistan is currently under a $7 billion International Monetary Fund bailout program and navigating a tricky path to recovery.

The South Asian nation narrowly averted a sovereign debt default, with reserves not sufficient enough to meet a month's worth of controlled imports.

The IFC had an exposure of $2.1 billion in Pakistan during the fiscal year 2024, ending in June, marking its record investment in the South Asian country's $350 billion economy.

Pakistan's economy grew by a meagre 0.92% in the first quarter of the fiscal year.

Diop said the IFC is looking into agriculture, infrastructure, the "very important" financial sector, and the digital sector.

Pakistan is looking to generate revenue by speeding up a privatisation push, but efforts to privatise the national flag carrier, Pakistan International Airlines , and outsource the capital's airport have fallen flat.

In line with the IFC's global push, Diop said equity-based transactions were to be expected in Pakistan too.

"Debt will still be a very important part in our business, but our equity will increase in the world, but also in Pakistan. It means we are believing really in Pakistan because we can take equity for a long, long time," he said.

data.title: Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary, Raise $3 Billion in IPO — Commodities Roundup
data.description:
MARKET MOVEMENTS:

  • Brent crude oil is down 0.2% at $74.84 a barrel
  • European benchmark gas is down 1.3% at 50.72 euros a megawatt-hour
  • Gold futures are down 1.4% at $2,905.20 a troy ounce
  • LME three-month copper futures are flat on $9,478.50 a metric ton

TOP STORY:

Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary, Raise $3 Billion in IPO

Eneos Holdings plans to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise as much as $3 billion through a listing that would be one of Japan's biggest such offerings in recent years.

The Japanese energy company said Friday that the Tokyo Stock Exchange approved the listing of unit JX Advanced Metals, scheduled for March 19.

Eneos said it would sell a majority of shares in the wholly owned subsidiary in the initial public offering, and would use the proceeds raised partly on investments necessary to increase the supply of next-generation energy such as synthetic fuels and hydrogen.

OTHER STORIES:

Why Your Valentine's Chocolate Is Getting More Expensive

This Valentine's Day marks a bittersweet time for chocolate lovers.

That is because of sky-high prices for chocolate's key ingredient, cocoa. While cocoa futures have retreated some from their latest record reached in December, they ended Thursday at $10,538 a metric ton. That is around 86% higher than a year ago and more than three times the typical price over the last two decades.

There are several reasons for cocoa's melt-up. Bad weather and plant diseases have wrecked crops in West Africa's growing regions, where aging cacao trees are becoming less productive. Meanwhile, a European Union deforestation law and low returns for growers have limited the planting of new groves. The extent of the supply issues means that prices are unlikely to return soon to the levels chocolate makers and consumers have been accustomed to paying, analysts say.

--

VP Bank Raises Industrial Metals Exposure as Inflation Hedge — Interview

The start of President Trump's second term has been a whirlwind for markets, presenting both risks and opportunities, and industrial metals and gold could be safe havens as hedges against potentially higher inflation, according to VP Bank.

"Don't bet only on U.S. equities," Felix Brill, chief investment officer at Liechtenstein-based VP Bank told The Wall Street Journal on Tuesday. "It all comes down to our investment philosophy: we don't like to have too many concentrated bets."

--

Copper Prices Jump as Market Weighs Odds of Fresh Trump Tariffs

Copper prices surged to three-month highs as the market's bets on the next round of President Trump's tariffs prompted a flurry of deliveries.

Three-month copper futures on the LME were up 1.5% at $9,618.0 a metric ton in European afternoon trading, having traded as high as $9,684.50 earlier in the session as traders scrambled to deliver large quantities of the metal to the U.S.

--

TC Energy Boosts Dividend; 4Q Earnings Fall After South Bow Spin Off

TC Energy nudged up its quarterly dividend in the latest quarter in anticipation of a further rise in underlying earnings this year and after spinning off its liquids pipelines business.

The Canadian energy company on Thursday reported income attributable to common shares of 971 million Canadian dollars ($684.1 million), or C$0.94 a share, for the fourth quarter, compared with C$1.46 billion, or C$1.41, a year earlier.

--

Enbridge Quarterly Earnings Squeezed by Unrealized Changes in Derivative Financial Instruments

Enbridge recorded a slump in earnings for the latest quarter as the pipeline operator and gas utility was squeezed by accounting changed on derivative instruments.

The Canadian energy company recorded earnings of 493 million Canadian dollars ($347.3 million), or C$0.23 a share, down sharply from C$1.73 billion, or C$0.81 a share, in the same period last year. On an adjusted basis, per-share earnings came in at C$0.75, in line with the mean forecast of analysts polled by FactSet.

--

NextEra Energy Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10%

NextEra Energy's board has raised the clean-energy company's quarterly dividend by 10%, to 56.65 cents from 51.5 cents.

--

Wood Group Shares Hit All-Time Low After Review Indicates Prior-Year Account Adjustments

John Wood Group shares hit a record low after the company said it was reviewing how much it would need to adjust prior-year earnings related to issues at its projects business, and saying it had a weaker-than-expected fourth quarter.

The FTSE 250-listed energy-services company also said Friday that it was in talks with lenders over financing options as most of its debt facilities are due to end in October 2026.

--

Dialog Shares Plunge After Surprise Loss

Dialog Group shares are set for their biggest decline in nearly two decades after reporting a quarterly loss.

Shares of the Malaysian oil-and-gas services provider plunged as much as 19% early Friday and were last down 18% at 1.48 ringgit. The shares are on track for their biggest one-day decline since 2008.

MARKET TALKS:

U.S. Aims to Cut Iran's Oil Flows, Treasury Secretary Bessent Says — Market Talk

1643 GMT - The U.S. aims to squeeze Iranian oil exports as part of its "maximum pressure" strategy on Tehran, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says in a Friday interview with Fox Business. "We are committed to bringing the Iranians to going back to the 100,000 barrels-a-day of oil exports," he says. Iran is currently exporting 1.5 million-1.6 million barrels per day, with export revenue used to fund terrorist activities, according to Bessent. "The Chinese, perhaps the Indians, are buying the sanctioned Iranian oil and that is unacceptable." Bessent says cutting Iran's oil exports and intensifying economic pressure will push the regime into severe economic distress. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Cattle Slips as Traders Eye Slower Slaughters — Market Talk

1041 ET - Live cattle futures are lower in early trading, with the most-active contract under pressure as traders mull the lower pace of slaughters. "It's meant packers haven't needed to be quite so aggressive in the cash markets," says StoneX in a note. "Heavier animal weights do continue to mute the slowdown in headcounts for total beef production metrics." The USDA will release its next Cattle on Feed report next Friday. Cattle is down 0.5%, whole lean hog futures are up 0.5%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

--

Oil Futures Appear Headed for Weekly Gains — Market Talk

1007 ET - Oil futures are higher in early trading and looking to post their first weekly gain in four weeks. The U.S. delay in imposing reciprocal trade tariffs globally and a slightly less bearish outlook from the IEA this week offset some of the downward pressure from a bigger-than-expected build in U.S. crude stocks and President Trump's talk with Russia's Vladimir Putin on starting negotiations to end the Russia-Ukraine war. Today traders focused on Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's comments about reducing Iran's exports to 100,000 barrels a day. WTI is up 0.4% at $71.56 a barrel and Brent is 0.6% higher at $75.47 a barrel. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Wheat Higher as Deep Cold Hits Plains — Market Talk

0937 ET - Winterkill concerns are supporting CBOT wheat futures, with the most-active contract up 2.3%. Highs in the Plains are expected to be well below freezing over the weekend, with subzero highs expected in North Dakota. Because of a lack of snow cover, traders are concerned about the bitter cold killing winter wheat crops. However, a storm system is also progressing across the U.S., according to a weather report from the USDA--which is expected to provide new snow cover for affected crops. Corn and soybeans are also higher, with corn up 0.7% and soybeans up 0.7%. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

--

U.S. Natural Gas Futures Head for Weekly Gain — Market Talk

0930 ET - U.S. natural gas futures are on track for a second straight weekly gain with the return of freezing weather this week and next supporting expectations of further inventory declines, while flows to LNG export facilities are at record highs. "Very cold mid-to-late February weather could send the storage deficit towards 300 Bcf in early March," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics says in a note. Natural gas inventories were 67 Bcf below their five-year average as of Feb. 7, according to the EIA's latest report. The Nymex front month is up 2.3% at $3.711/mmBtu. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

--

Brazil Ethanol Industry Group Protests Trump Tariff Threats — Market Talk

0921 ET - The wave of reciprocal tariffs proposed by President Trump is raising the ire of trading partners globally. On the green energy side, Brazilian ethanol producers are protesting the potential measures, arguing that they 'make no sense' when comparing U.S. and Brazilian ethanol. "The measure aims to put ethanol produced in Brazil and the United States on the same level, even though they have different environmental attributes and decarbonization potential, and therefore it makes no sense to talk about reciprocity," says UNICA, a Brazilian trade group representing the sugarcane and renewable energy industries. U.S. ethanol uses corn as its main feedstock, while Brazilian ethanol uses sugarcane. Ethanol is a major consumer of corn for U.S. producers. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

--

Wheat, Corn Prices Rise on Latest USDA Report — Market Talk 1300 GMT - Agricultural commodity prices rise, with Chicago wheat futures up 2.3% at $5.91 a bushel and corn futures up 0.9% at $4.98 a bushel. Wheat is trading at its highest price since late October, after the latest U.S. Department of Agriculture report confirmed global wheat and corn estimates for 2025, Commerzbank says. Wheat consumption forecasts in the world's biggest consumer China has been raised slightly, meaning a fall in expectations for stock levels by year end, Commerzbank says. However, outside of China, wheat stocks in major exporting countries have ticked up. For corn, there has been a cut to expectations for global stock levels for the end of the year, with lower-than-expected production and current stock levels, Commerzbank says. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Gold Futures Rise, But Correction Potential Also Gaining, Commerzbank Says — Market Talk

1158 GMT - Gold futures rise to near-record levels, though the high price is starting to look overdone, Commerzbank says. Futures are up 0.4% at $2,957.80 a troy ounce, just below the record $2,968.50 an ounce set on Tuesday. The market is reacting asymmetrically to the fresh records, as current developments in the war in Ukraine and lower U.S. interest rate cut expectations--which should be reasonable headwinds--failed to slow gold's rise, Commerzbank's Carsten Fritsch says. President Trump's tariff announcements, however, provided immediate price boost, Fritsch says in a note. Other important influences like the U.S. dollar and bond yields play only minor roles, Fritsch adds. It is difficult to say how long the unusual situation will last. In the short-term, gold could reach $3,000, but this will increase correction potential, Fritsch adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Base Metal Prices Rise; Copper Spreads Widen in U.S. on Tariff Threat — Market Talk

1047 GMT - Base metal prices rise, with LME three-month copper up 1.6% at $9,626 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum up 1.1% at $2,637 a ton. President Trump has set out plans for so-called reciprocal tariffs on a country-by-country basis, with additional levies on top of aluminum and steel tariffs. The administration hasn't set out plans on when copper levies could be defined or implemented, says Britannia Global Markets' Neil Welsh. The move is seen as a potential opening bid for negotiation, Welsh writes, as Trump believes the trading environment is imbalanced. The tariff risk is disrupting the global copper market with copper spreads under threat, Welsh says in a note. Copper on U.S. commodities exchange Comex is up around 19% since the start of the year, compared with around 9.5% on the LME. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

--

Palm Oil Rises on Positive Sentiment — Market Talk

1014 GMT - Palm oil prices closed higher, tracking overnight gains in soybean oil on the Chicago Board of Trade. Strength in the rival soy oil market, as well as positive sentiment surrounding Malaysia's declining palm oil stocks, likely supported prices, analysts at Kenanga Futures write in a note. The recent rally has also been driven by tighter inventories, and markets expect an upcoming seasonal rise in export demand, they add. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for April delivery rose MYR41 to MYR4,595/ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

--

Europe's Gas Price Drops Below 50-Euro Mark — Market Talk

0945 GMT - European natural-gas prices extend losses in early trade, dropping below the 50-euros-a-megawatt-hour mark amid talks to end the war in Ukraine and discussions to ease EU storage targets. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract is down 4.1% to 49.30 euros a megawatt hour and on track to end the week 11% lower. A peace deal between Russia and Ukraine would ease fears of supply disruptions and potentially allow the resumption of Russian flows, further pushing down prices. Meanwhile, some EU countries expressed concerns about EU gas-refilling rules and rising prices, with Germany calling for exemptions from the targets this year, according to a Bloomberg report. Cold weather and low wind-power generation have pushed the EU to dip into its storage faster than expected in recent weeks, raising concerns over refilling requirements over the summer. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Oil Rises After Trump Delays Reciprocal Tariffs — Market Talk

0918 GMT - Oil prices edge higher in early trade after U.S. President Trump delayed immediate plans to impose reciprocal tariffs, easing fears of a trade war that could hurt global growth and demand for crude. Brent is up 0.6% at $75.48 a barrel, while WTI rises 0.5% to $71.64 a barrel. Trump's decision to have federal agencies study how to adjust tariffs to match those of other countries by April 1 provided some temporary relief to the market and pushed down the U.S. dollar. Meanwhile, the IEA lifted its oil-demand growth forecast and lowered its expectations for a global oil surplus this year, providing further support to market sentiment. Still, gains continue to be capped by persistent fears of escalating trade tensions and prospects of a potential peace deal between Russia and Ukraine. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

--

Comex Gold Futures Return to Bullish Trajectory, Chart Shows — Market Talk

0627 GMT - Comex gold futures have returned to the bullish trajectory, based on daily the chart, RHB Retail Research's Joseph Chai says in a research report. Thursday's price movements show the precious metal underwent strong buying pressure, with the potential to maintain the uptrend toward the $3,000/oz level, the analyst says. The relative strength index is also pointing upward, supporting positive price movements with an acceleration in bullish momentum, Chai says. Should the commodity breach $3,000/oz, it will probably trend higher toward the $3,100/oz level, the analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.2% higher at $2,932.93/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

--

Iron Ore Futures Rise But Investors' Sentiment Remain Weak — Market Talk

0346 GMT - Iron ore futures are higher in Asian trade. While fundamentals of the black metal are solid, market sentiment is weak due to a lack of strong demand, Nanhua Futures analysts write in a note. Analysts at Everbright Futures note that current inventory levels of iron ore remain high and additional tariffs on steel and aluminum from the U.S. are weighing on sentiment in the short term. Investors should keep an eye on geopolitical tensions and macro news, they say. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is up 0.6% at CNY810.5/ton. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Tech Turbulence: Intel's Market Share Dives as AMD and Arm Surge
data.description:
Feb 14 - In Q4 of 2024, Intel (INTC, Financial) confronts challenges in the microprocessor market with AMD (AMD, Financial) and Arm Holdings (ARM, Financial) bolstering their respective positions in the segment. According to data from Mercury Research, total MPU shipments increased 10.8% compared sequentially, as notebooks and servers are enjoying strong demand. Strong orders boost AMD's unit share from 21.6% to 22.1% across both segments, and accelerating consumer notebook shipments lift Arm's share from 10% to 10.5%. However, Intel registers a 1.4% point dip to 67.4% market share. While Intel's desktop shipments have grown almost two percentage points to 68.1%, shortcomings in notebook and server performance have given its overall share a downturn.

Intel shares were down about 3%, and Arm stocks slipped by 5% on Friday morning, signaling a market correction amid excess inventory and uneven global demand. This decline indicates a need for strategic adjustments rather than a sign of steady progress.

data.title: Toronto Stocks Retreat; CAE Shares Rise on New Board Appointments
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Toronto indexes were firmly lower Friday mid-trading, dragged down by most sectors trending lower. Materials, consumer services and health-tech stocks were the main drags of the session, with only consumer service posting any notable gains, followed more distantly by health services and tech stocks.

Canadian manufacturing activity continued to pick up into the end of last year, rising 0.3% in December, thanks to a drive in sales from a weakening Canadian dollar and better petroleum trade.

Canada's S&P/TSX Composite Index fell 0.4% to 25596.3 and blue-chip S&P/TSX 60 declined by 0.5% to 1539.44.

Shares of CAE rose nearly 17% to 38.91 Canadian dollars ($27.41) after the flight-training company made changes to its board, appointing four new directors, including Air Canada's former CEO as chair of the board, while it searches for its next chief executive.

Other market movers:

Shares in Magna International fell by 4.2% to C$53.88 after the auto-parts maker said it expects lower sales over the next two years, below analyst forecasts of growth, as the auto industry faces challenges and uncertainty.

Enbridge shares fell 3% to C$61.74 after the pipeline operator and gas utility reported a slump in earnings for the latest quarter as it was squeezed by accounting changes on derivative instruments.

Shares of TC Energy fell by 3.1% to C$65.11. The company bumped up its quarterly dividend in the latest quarter in anticipation of a further rise in underlying earnings this year and after it spinned off its liquids pipelines business.

Shares of MTY Food Group fell 9.1% to C$45.50 after the company reported a loss in the fourth quarter due to an impairment charge on one of its brand assets as well as large foreign-exchange losses.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com
